providerExtensions.name: Hook Event Set
providerExtensions.description: Claude Code supports 24 distinct hook events covering the full session lifecycle: SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PermissionRequest, PermissionDenied, PostToolUse, PostToolUseFailure, Notification, SubagentStart, SubagentStop, TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, Stop, StopFailure, TeammateIdle, InstructionsLoaded, ConfigChange, CwdChanged, FileChanged, WorktreeCreate, WorktreeRemove, PreCompact, PostCompact, Elicitation, ElicitationResult, and SessionEnd. Events fall into three cadences: once per session (SessionStart, SessionEnd), once per turn (UserPromptSubmit, Stop, StopFailure), and on every tool call inside the agentic loop (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, etc.). Some events (UserPromptSubmit, Stop, TeammateIdle, TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, WorktreeCreate, WorktreeRemove, CwdChanged) do not support matchers and always fire on every occurrence.

providerExtensions.name: Four Hook Handler Types
providerExtensions.description: Each hook handler can be one of four types. (1) command (type: "command"): runs a shell command; event JSON arrives on stdin; exit code 0 means success, exit code 2 is a blocking error, any other non-zero is a non-blocking error; response via stdout JSON or stderr. (2) http (type: "http"): POSTs JSON to a URL; 2xx with JSON body uses same response schema as command hooks; non-2xx or connection errors are non-blocking. (3) prompt (type: "prompt"): single-turn LLM evaluation; model returns {"ok": true/false, "reason": "..."}; defaults to a fast model (Haiku). (4) agent (type: "agent"): spawns a subagent with tool access (Read, Grep, Glob, up to 50 turns) that returns the same ok/reason schema. Prompt and agent hooks are only supported on a subset of events (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PostToolUseFailure, PermissionRequest, Stop, SubagentStop, TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, UserPromptSubmit). SessionStart only supports command hooks. All other events support command and http only.

providerExtensions.name: Hook Scopes and Locations
providerExtensions.description: Hooks can be defined at six scopes: user (~/.claude/settings.json, all projects, not shareable), project (.claude/settings.json, single project, can be committed to repo), local (.claude/settings.local.json, single project, gitignored), managed policy (organization-wide, admin-controlled), plugin (hooks/hooks.json bundled with a plugin), and component frontmatter (skill or agent YAML frontmatter, active only while that component is running). Enterprise administrators can use allowManagedHooksOnly to block user, project, and plugin hooks; plugins force-enabled in managed settings enabledPlugins are exempt. disableAllHooks in user/project/local settings cannot disable managed hooks; only disableAllHooks at the managed settings level can.

providerExtensions.name: Matcher Pattern Syntax
providerExtensions.description: The matcher field in a hook group filters when the group fires. Evaluation depends on characters: "*", "", or omitted matches all; a value containing only letters, digits, underscores, and pipe characters is treated as an exact string or pipe-separated list of exact strings (e.g., "Edit|Write"); any other characters are treated as a JavaScript regular expression (e.g., "^Notebook", "mcp__memory__.*"). Each event type matches on a different field: tool events match on tool_name, SessionStart matches on source (startup, resume, clear, compact), SessionEnd matches on reason, Notification matches on notification_type, etc. FileChanged does not use regex for its watch list; matcher values are split on | and registered as literal filenames to watch in the current directory.

providerExtensions.name: Decision Control via Exit Codes and JSON Output
providerExtensions.description: Hooks communicate decisions through two mechanisms. Exit codes: exit 0 means success (stdout JSON is parsed); exit 2 is a blocking error (stderr fed to Claude or user, action blocked if the event supports it); any other non-zero is a non-blocking error (transcript shows an error notice, execution continues). JSON output (on exit 0): universal fields include "continue" (false stops Claude entirely), "stopReason", "suppressOutput", "systemMessage"; event-specific fields vary. PreToolUse uses hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision ("allow"/"deny"/"ask"/"defer") with optional updatedInput (modifies tool parameters before execution) and additionalContext. PermissionRequest uses hookSpecificOutput.decision.behavior ("allow"/"deny"). PostToolUse and Stop use top-level "decision": "block". WorktreeCreate hooks must print the new worktree path on stdout. HTTP hooks use 2xx+JSON body for structured control; non-2xx is always non-blocking regardless of body content.

providerExtensions.name: Async (Background) Hook Execution
providerExtensions.description: Command hooks support "async": true to run in the background without blocking Claude. Async hooks cannot return decisions (decision, permissionDecision, continue fields are ignored because the triggering action has already proceeded). systemMessage and additionalContext outputs are delivered to Claude on the next conversation turn. Only type: "command" hooks support async; prompt-based hooks cannot run asynchronously. Each async execution creates a separate background process with no deduplication. The timeout field sets max background process time (same 10-minute default as sync hooks if not specified). Completion notifications are suppressed by default; enable with Ctrl+O or --verbose.

providerExtensions.name: PreToolUse Input Modification and Defer
providerExtensions.description: PreToolUse hooks can modify a tool's input parameters before execution via the updatedInput field in hookSpecificOutput. The entire input object is replaced, so all fields (changed and unchanged) must be included. Input modification can be combined with any permissionDecision value except "defer". The "defer" decision value (requires CC v2.1.89+) is for non-interactive mode (-p flag): it exits Claude with stop_reason: "tool_deferred" so a calling process (e.g. Agent SDK app) can collect input through its own UI and resume with --resume. Defer only works when Claude makes a single tool call in the turn; it is ignored with a warning if multiple tool calls are batched.

providerExtensions.name: Persistent Environment Variable Propagation via CLAUDE_ENV_FILE
providerExtensions.description: SessionStart, CwdChanged, and FileChanged hooks have access to the CLAUDE_ENV_FILE environment variable, which provides a path to a file where export statements can be written. Variables written to this file persist into subsequent Bash commands for the remainder of the session. Use append (>>) to preserve variables from other hooks. CwdChanged and FileChanged hooks can also return a "watchPaths" field in their JSON output to dynamically update which file paths FileChanged watches; returning an empty array clears the dynamic watch list.

providerExtensions.name: Component-Scoped Hooks in Skills and Agent Frontmatter
providerExtensions.description: Hooks can be defined directly in skill or subagent YAML frontmatter. These hooks are scoped to the component's lifecycle: they only run while that skill or subagent is active and are cleaned up when it finishes. All hook events are supported. For subagents, Stop hooks in frontmatter are automatically converted to SubagentStop events at runtime. Skills support an additional "once": true field on handlers, which causes the hook to fire only once per session then be removed. The hooks field in component frontmatter uses the same configuration format as settings-based hooks.

providerExtensions.name: PermissionRequest Permission Update Entries
providerExtensions.description: PermissionRequest hooks can return updatedPermissions in their hookSpecificOutput to apply permission rule changes when allowing a request. Entry types: addRules (adds allow/ deny/ask rules for tool+pattern combinations), replaceRules (replaces all rules of a given behavior at a destination), removeRules (removes matching rules), setMode (changes the session permission mode to default/acceptEdits/dontAsk/bypassPermissions/plan), and addDirectories/removeDirectories (manages working directories). Each entry has a destination that controls persistence: "session" (in-memory only), "localSettings", "projectSettings", or "userSettings". A hook can echo permission_suggestions from its input as updatedPermissions, equivalent to the user selecting that "always allow" option in the dialog.

providerExtensions.name: PostToolUse MCP Tool Output Override
providerExtensions.description: PostToolUse hooks for MCP tools can return an "updatedMCPToolOutput" field that replaces the tool's output with the provided value before Claude sees it. This allows hooks to filter, transform, or annotate MCP tool results. Only applies to MCP tools; has no effect on internal Claude Code tools.

providerExtensions.name: WorktreeCreate Hook Replaces Default Git Behavior
providerExtensions.description: When a WorktreeCreate hook is configured, it completely replaces Claude Code's default git worktree creation. The hook must return the absolute path to the created worktree directory (command hooks print it on stdout; HTTP hooks return it via hookSpecificOutput.worktreePath). This enables non-git VCS support (SVN, Perforce, Mercurial). Because .worktreeinclude is not processed when a hook replaces default behavior, copying local config files (.env, etc.) into the new worktree must be done inside the hook script. WorktreeCreate: any non-zero exit code (not just 2) causes worktree creation to fail. WorktreeRemove hook fires on cleanup; failures are logged in debug mode only.

providerExtensions.name: Elicitation and ElicitationResult Hooks
providerExtensions.description: The Elicitation event fires when an MCP server requests user input mid-task. Hooks can intercept and respond programmatically via hookSpecificOutput with action ("accept", "decline", "cancel") and content (form field values for accept), bypassing the interactive dialog. Supports form mode (server defines a JSON schema for form fields) and URL mode (browser-based authentication flow). ElicitationResult fires after the user responds to an elicitation; hooks can observe, modify, or block the response before it is sent back to the MCP server. Exit code 2 on either event denies/blocks the elicitation. Both events match on MCP server name.
